data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit вЂ” parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) Г— child B6 (7e48: Remove throwaway sample workflows; confirm two-real-workflow end state)]

Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B5 f12d, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumptions, corroborating the prior G4 audit on this pairing). The other six modes read benign (footnoted). Independently re-verified against the live tree.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
FINDING 1 вЂ” Conflicting assumptions: B6 (CLOSED+SIGNED) attests the end-state as "the gates run on the two real [engine] workflows" (Why/What + AC3), but on the live tree the plan-review gate still dispatches through the BESPOKE path (verify.gate_engine default reverted to "bespoke" by PR #42), and a still-OPEN sibling (0408) is what re-defaults plan-review onto the workflow engine. So B6 signs off the "two real LLM workflows run on engine" end-state while a sibling establishes that one of those two gates is not yet running on the engine live.
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
- finding: B6's signed end-state confirmation overreaches the live state вЂ” it reads as the engine-substrate end-state being reached, but plan-review runs on bespoke until 0408 lands. B6's normative ACs are actually only FILE INVENTORY (delete code_review.yaml + templates.py; retain review_skeleton.yaml; exactly two gate YAMLs + one sample), which IS sound and verified; the inconsistency is confined to the "and the gates RUN on the two real workflows" phrasing in Why/What/AC3.
- criteria: [parent AC6 / Success Criterion ("End state: exactly two real LLM workflows (plan-review, completion-verification) ... run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate"); B6 Why/What ("Confirm ... that the gates run on the two real workflows") + B6 AC3; 0408 AC4 ("verify.gate_engine default is flipped back to workflow") + 0408 AC2 (live review-plan returns a real PASS/BLOCK via the workflow engine)]
- location: B6 Why/What + AC3; src/rebar/config.py gate_engine default (reverted to "bespoke" per PR #42, per 0408 Why + B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001); the workflow plan-review verify/coach prompt steps (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py RunnerAgentStep; gates/plan-review.yaml).
- evidence: ["B6 is CLOSED+SIGNED; its What/AC3 frame the end-state as the gates running on the two real (engine) workflows.", "Live default is bespoke: 0408 Why states PR #42 'flipped the default back to bespoke as a hotfix' because the workflow plan-review verify/coach steps fail to resolve {{plan}} live в†’ INDETERMINATE (RunnerAgentStep supplies only ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path); B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001 confirms 'bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate.'", "B6's own claim comment concedes it is 'based on the hotfix branch (fix/gate-engine-default-bespoke) since it must merge after #42' вЂ” i.e. B6 lands on top of a tree where plan-review runs bespoke, yet AC3 declares the two-real-workflow run-on-engine end-state confirmed.", "B6's completion-verification PASS read AC3 strictly as FILE INVENTORY ('No other .yaml files under examples/ or gates/'); it did NOT verify plan-review actually runs on its engine workflow live, so the 'gates run on the two real workflows' half of B6's stated intent is unsubstantiated against the live default."]
- scenarios: ["A reader trusts B6 (signed, AC3 'end state confirmed') as proof the epic's two-real-workflow-on-engine end-state holds, not realizing plan-review still dispatches through bespoke until 0408 lands вЂ” so the parent Success Criterion is presented as met while not yet true on the live path.", "If 0408 is deprioritized/dropped, the epic ships with B6 signed-confirming the end-state while gate_engine stays 'bespoke' вЂ” plan-review never actually runs on the engine substrate live, silently defeating the parent's central goal despite a green/signed B6.", "B-RETIRE (961b), which removes the bespoke path, is gated on 0408 (its own comment); B6's signed 'end state confirmed' could be mistaken as evidence the engine path is already the working gate, tempting premature bespoke removal."]
- impact: The sibling set is internally inconsistent on the load-bearing end-state: B6's signed AC3 reads as the engine-substrate end-state being reached, but the live tree has plan-review on bespoke (0408 still open to re-default to workflow). Same root cause as the B2/0408 {{plan}}-plumbing gap. The file-inventory part of B6 (delete code_review.yaml + templates.py; retain review_skeleton.yaml; exactly two gate YAMLs + one sample) is sound and verified; only the "and the gates RUN on the two real workflows" phrasing overreaches.
- checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes ... conflicting assumptions ... each detected mode is a finding."
- suggested_fix: Scope B6's confirmation to INVENTORY and decouple it from the run-on-engine claim: (a) reword B6's Why/What + AC3 to "exactly two real LLM workflow FILES exist (plan-review.yaml, completion-verification.yaml) + the one retained sample; no throwaway stubs remain," and drop/qualify "the gates run on the two real workflows" so B6's signature does not assert a live-engine end-state it did not verify; (b) cross-reference that "plan-review runs on the engine substrate LIVE (default=workflow)" is delivered by 0408 (tepid-bus-pomp), not B6; (c) key the parent's AC6 end-state checkbox off 0408's completion (default re-flipped to workflow), not B6's inventory confirmation.

в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state вЂ” B6 mutates shared surfaces: src/rebar/llm/workflow/__init__.py (drop templates re-exports), _cli/_workflow_commands.py (inline the scaffold), and the bound test files (test_cli.py, test_code_review_example.py, test_workflow_schema_golden.py, test_bpmn.py). No sibling concurrently mutates these post-cutover; B6 sequences itself after B5/PR #42 on the hotfix branch. Acknowledged, not hidden.

(2) Conflicting assumptions (other) вЂ” captured as Finding 1.

(3) Dependency gap вЂ” B6 depends on B5 ("cutover complete, so the bespoke samples are truly unused"). Minor rationale mismatch: code_review.yaml + templates.py are throwaway EXAMPLE/scaffold stubs, not the bespoke plan-review path, so their deletion does not strictly require the gate cutover вЂ” but B6's real gating concern (merge AFTER PR #42 on the hotfix branch, per its claim comment) is sound, and B-RETIRE (961b) correctly KEEPS passes.py etc. that the workflow still uses. No actual missing dependency. (Verified: templates.scaffold is consumed only by `rebar workflow new` via _workflow_commands.py:224 + __init__.py:67 re-export + test_cli.py; code_review.yaml by test_bpmn.py:398, test_workflow_schema_golden.py:78, test_code_review_example.py:24, README.md:164. B6 lists all of these in file_impact and preserves `rebar workflow new` via an inline scaffold.)

(4) Scope overlap вЂ” B6 RETAINS examples/review_skeleton.yaml; B-UX (92fa) / B-DM (5506) CONSUME it as the visual-editing refinement sample. Distinct ownership (retain vs. consume), not duplicated. Parent AC5's "epic A's demo workflow" maps correctly to examples/review_skeleton.yaml. Benign.

(5) Ordering violation вЂ” B6 depends on B5 and blocks B-RETIRE (961b) (dep present: 7e48 blocks 961b). Correctly positioned after cutover, before bespoke-path removal. Consistent.

(6) Consumer impact вЂ” B6 deletes templates.py (consumed by `rebar workflow new` via _workflow_commands.py:224 + __init__.py re-export; also schema.py:424 docstring + README.md:164 reference the COMMAND, not the deleted files) and code_review.yaml (consumed by the three test files above). B6 explicitly preserves `rebar workflow new` via an inline scaffold and lists all bound test files + __init__.py in file_impact. Note: the deleted SCAFFOLD_V1 referenced the `code-quality` prompt + `gate`/`fetch_ticket` ops вЂ” the inline replacement must remain lint-clean (the CLI self-checks it via lint_workflow), which the completion verification implies holds. No unhandled consumer break.

(7) Residual references вЂ” B6 AC2 requires grep-clean; its completion-verification asserts no live references to code_review.yaml/templates.py/SCAFFOLD_V1 remain (the only hit is a documenting comment inside review_skeleton.yaml). The retained review_skeleton.yaml is preserved, so no dangling reference to a removed symbol. Benign.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because the live tree OBJECTIVELY contradicts B6's signed end-state framing вЂ” gate_engine default was reverted to "bespoke" (PR #42; corroborated by 0408 Why + B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001 "bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate"), and B6's own claim comment concedes it merges on top of fix/gate-engine-default-bespoke вЂ” so "the gates run on the two real [engine] workflows" is not true live, while a separate open sibling (0408) owns flipping it back. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule. This corroborates the prior G4 audit (1782508810906868001) on the same pairing.
